notes: MRT-3 feeder routes compiled from Wikipedia, Moovit, Rome2rio, commutetour.com, ph-commute.com, and other web sources. Each route object represents a jeepney/bus feeder connecting to an MRT-3 station. 'origin' and 'destination' reflect the full route endpoints; the MRT station is an intermediate stop or the terminal point of the feeder.

routes.route_name: Dapitan – Pasay RTDA via L. Guinto
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service; frequency not documented
routes.notes: Serves Taft Ave MRT Station area along L. Guinto corridor

routes.route_name: Blumentritt – Pasay RTDA via Quiapo, L. Guinto
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: Manila north to Pasay corridor via Quiapo and L. Guinto

routes.route_name: Baclaran – Blumentritt via L. Guinto (Quiapo variant)
routes.frequency_notes: q5 min approximate
routes.notes: Major north-south jeepney on L. Guinto/Taft corridor

routes.route_name: Baclaran – Retiro via L. Guinto, Quiapo
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: Extends past Blumentritt toward Retiro

routes.route_name: Divisoria – Pasay RTDA via L. Guinto
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: Links Divisoria market district to Taft/Pasay area

routes.route_name: Alabang (Starmall) – Pasay RTDA via Expressway
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service from Pasay Taft terminal
routes.notes: Southern Metro Manila to Taft MRT via expressway

routes.route_name: UBE Express – Araneta Cubao ↔ NAIA (via Pasay/Taft area)
routes.frequency_notes: Operates from Victory Liner terminal near Taft MRT
routes.notes: Airport feeder from Taft area; Victory Liner terminal walkable from Taft MRT

routes.route_name: PRC sign jeepney – Chino Roces Ave. (San Lorenzo Place to PRC/Home Depot)
routes.frequency_notes: q15 min (Sabio St/Chino Roces departure); 10-min journey
routes.notes: PRC, Mantrade sign jeepneys from San Lorenzo Place side. Also catches 'ECO Plaza' sign from Alphaland Southgate Mall on opposite lane

routes.route_name: FTI – Kayamanan C via Chino Roces / C5
routes.frequency_notes: First trip from Innove Makati noted at 4:08 PM (may be limited hours)
routes.notes: Links Magallanes MRT catchment to Taguig via Chino Roces Ave. and C5

routes.route_name: Ayala – Mantrade via Pasong Tamo
routes.frequency_notes: q15 min
routes.notes: Northbound jeepney from Chino Roces area toward Ayala; passes Magallanes MRT catchment

routes.route_name: BGC Bus – EDSA Terminal (Ayala) to BGC
routes.origin: BGC Bus EDSA Terminal, Ayala MRT
routes.destination: BGC (various stops: McKinley, 30th St, Lawton Ave.)
routes.frequency_notes: High frequency; BGC Bus documented separately in bgc-bus.md
routes.notes: BGC Bus EDSA Terminal at Ayala MRT is the primary access point for BGC from EDSA rail

routes.route_name: EDSA Carousel – Ayala stop
routes.frequency_notes: Peak: q3-5 min; off-peak: q10 min; 24/7
routes.notes: Flat fare ₱15. Ayala stop among busiest Carousel boarding points. Northbound and southbound

routes.route_name: Guadalupe – Cartimar jeepney via Gil Puyat Ave.
routes.frequency_notes: q5 min; 4-min journey segment near station
routes.notes: Traverses entire length of Gil Puyat Ave. east-west; primary Buendia station feeder

routes.route_name: Salcedo Village Electric Jeepney
routes.frequency_notes: Regular city e-jeepney; frequency not documented
routes.notes: Makati City-operated electric jeepney for CBD last-mile access; Salcedo Village variant

routes.route_name: Legaspi Village Electric Jeepney
routes.frequency_notes: Regular city e-jeepney; frequency not documented
routes.notes: Makati City-operated electric jeepney for CBD last-mile access; Legaspi Village variant

routes.route_name: eSakay Electric Jeepney – Circuit Makati / Mandaluyong City Hall
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service; exact frequency not documented
routes.notes: eSakay e-jeepney brand; extends Buendia MRT catchment to Mandaluyong and Circuit Makati

routes.route_name: Ayala Loop Jeepney – Paseo de Roxas (northbound only)
routes.frequency_notes: Northbound only; regular service
routes.notes: One-way northbound loop serving Paseo de Roxas corridor for Makati CBD access

routes.route_name: Route 202: Fort Bonifacio Gate 3 – Guadalupe / Market Market (ABC)
routes.destination: Guadalupe Jeep Terminal / Market Market ABC
routes.frequency_notes: q10 min; 38-min journey (full route); 4 AM – 11 PM (modern jeep); 24h (regular jeep)
routes.notes: Primary BGC-access jeepney from Guadalupe MRT. Boards near Jollibee at Guadalupe terminal

routes.route_name: T266: AFP/PNP Taguig – Guadalupe (ABC) via M. Asuncion
routes.destination: EDSA Central Jeepney Terminal, Mandaluyong / Guadalupe MRT
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service; frequency not documented
routes.notes: Serves military/police housing in Taguig toward Guadalupe MRT and EDSA

routes.route_name: FTI / Kayamanan-C – Guadalupe via C5
routes.frequency_notes: 4 AM – 11 PM (modern jeep); 24h (regular); some night service
routes.notes: Important route to FTI industrial area and Taguig residential areas along C5

routes.route_name: M. Almeda, Pateros → Market Market C-5 Driveway
routes.destination: Market Market C-5 Driveway, BGC, Taguig
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: Links Pateros municipality to BGC via C5 corridor; passes through Guadalupe MRT catchment area

routes.route_name: Mandaluyong – RTU (Rizal Technological University) via Boni Ave.
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service; frequency not documented
routes.notes: Primary documented jeepney feeder from Boni Station; serves RTU student commuters

routes.route_name: Shaw Blvd – Pasig jeepney via Parklea Centre exit
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service; frequency not documented
routes.notes: Exit via Parklea Centre (4th exit, not Shangri-La side) to access Pasig-bound jeepney terminal

routes.route_name: EDSA Carousel – Ortigas stop
routes.frequency_notes: Peak: q3-5 min; off-peak: q10 min; 24/7
routes.notes: Ortigas is a major stop on the EDSA Carousel BRT

routes.route_name: Jeepneys along Ortigas Ave. (east-west corridor)
routes.destination: Cainta / Taytay, Rizal (eastbound) OR Mandaluyong/San Juan (westbound)
routes.frequency_notes: q5-15 min depending on specific route
routes.notes: Ortigas Ave. corridor serves Pasig City and Rizal Province destinations

routes.route_name: EDSA Annapolis–Greenhills Shopping Center
routes.frequency_notes: q15 min; 4-min journey
routes.notes: Primary feeder from Santolan-Annapolis MRT to Greenhills. Jeepney terminal at EDSA-Annapolis corner

routes.route_name: Gate 5 – Greenhills Shopping Center Loop
routes.frequency_notes: Regular loop; frequency not documented
routes.notes: Dedicated loop connecting Camp Crame area to Greenhills; also serves MRT station

routes.route_name: Gate5–Unimart (LTFRB)
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: LTFRB-regulated jeepney serving inner San Juan residential areas to Greenhills

routes.route_name: Greenhills Wilson E-Trike (launched Dec 2024)
routes.frequency_notes: Fleet of 10 e-trikes; special franchise (City Ordinance No. 32, s-2024); launched December 2024
routes.notes: New San Juan City e-trike service providing last-mile from Greenhills area. Complements MRT Santolan-Annapolis catchment

routes.route_name: EDSA Carousel – Santolan stop
routes.frequency_notes: Peak: q3-5 min; off-peak: q10 min; 24/7
routes.notes: Santolan is a stop on the EDSA Carousel BRT

routes.route_name: UBE Express – Araneta City Bus Port ↔ NAIA
routes.frequency_notes: q30 min peak; regular service airport hours
routes.notes: Key airport P2P feeder from Cubao MRT-3/LRT-2 hub. Flat ₱150 fare

routes.route_name: QCityBus Route 1 – QC Hall → Cubao
routes.frequency_notes: Free; peak q12 min, off-peak q20 min (see qcitybus-routes.md for detail)
routes.notes: QC Government free bus; terminates at/near Araneta Center

routes.route_name: LRT-2 Transfer at Araneta Center–Cubao Station
routes.frequency_notes: LRT-2 service hours 5AM–9:30PM
routes.notes: Rail-to-rail transfer via ~400m covered walkway through Gateway Mall/Farmer's Plaza. Most significant intermodal transfer on MRT-3

routes.route_name: España Rotonda–Proj. 2 & 3 via Timog
key_stops: Tomas Morato Ave. / Scout De Guia
routes.frequency_notes: q5 min; 9-min journey (Kamias–Tomas Morato segment)
routes.notes: Key Tomas Morato feeder from GMA-Kamuning MRT area; very frequent service

routes.route_name: Cubao via Timog jeepney
routes.destination: Cubao (via Timog Ave. then Ybardolaza St.)
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: Turns left on Ybardolaza St. en route to Cubao; passes Timog Ave. entertainment district

routes.route_name: EDSA Carousel – Kamuning stop (exclusive to MRT station, opened July 15 2024)
routes.frequency_notes: Peak: q3-5 min; off-peak: q10 min; 24/7
routes.notes: Kamuning EDSA Carousel stop is accessible ONLY through the MRT station — no separate street-level stop. Opened July 15, 2024

routes.route_name: JAM Liner – Southern Luzon (near GMA-Kamuning)
routes.origin: JAM Liner terminal near EDSA-East Ave/Timog junction
routes.frequency_notes: Multiple daily departures (provincial schedule)
routes.notes: JAM Liner terminal just south of EDSA at East Ave./Timog intersection; JAC Liner also nearby

routes.route_name: Eton Centris jeepney terminal – Fairview-bound jeepneys
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service; q15 min approximate
routes.notes: Jeepney terminal inside Eton Centris mall at Quezon Ave. MRT provides Fairview access without going to North Ave.

routes.route_name: UP Diliman campus 'MRT' internal route
routes.frequency_notes: Regular university service; see lrt2-feeder-routes.md Katipunan section for full UP campus route detail
routes.notes: One of six internal UP campus routes; explicitly terminates at Quezon Ave. MRT. Green roof color coding

routes.route_name: EDSA Carousel – Quezon Ave. stop
routes.frequency_notes: Peak: q3-5 min; off-peak: q10 min; 24/7
routes.notes: Quezon Ave. is a regular stop on the EDSA Carousel BRT

routes.route_name: Fairview-bound jeepneys from North Avenue / SM North Terminal
routes.origin: North Avenue MRT / SM North EDSA Terminal
routes.frequency_notes: q15 min; 1h 16min journey; first trip 6:02 AM, last 12:02 AM
routes.notes: SM North EDSA Terminal operates 5 AM–10 PM. North Ave. MRT is the primary EDSA rail entry point for Fairview commuters

routes.route_name: Marcos Ave. – Quirino Hi-way via T. Sora (to Novaliches)
routes.frequency_notes: First trip ~7:00 AM; last ~1:19 AM
routes.notes: Primary Novaliches-bound feeder from North Avenue MRT

routes.route_name: Don Mariano Marcos Ave. – Quirino Hwy via T. Sora
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: Alternative Novaliches route via Don Mariano Marcos Ave.

routes.route_name: Lagro – Rizal Ave. via A. Bonifacio
routes.frequency_notes: Regular service
routes.notes: Connects Lagro (far northwest QC) to Manila via North Ave. MRT corridor

routes.route_name: EDSA Carousel – North Avenue northbound terminus
routes.frequency_notes: Peak: q3-5 min; off-peak: q10 min; 24/7
routes.notes: North Avenue is the northern terminus of the EDSA Carousel BRT

routes.route_name: Bulacan-bound buses from North Ave. EDSA
routes.frequency_notes: q5 min (EDSA northbound bus frequency); SM North EDSA Terminal 5 AM–10 PM
routes.notes: Buses from SM North EDSA Terminal serving Bulacan province; also serves as Novaliches express access
